prompt:
You are an expert LLVM compiler engineer, specializing in LLVM IR, InstCombine, and missed-optimization analysis.

I found a **new missed optimization in InstCombine** and currently have **only one LLVM IR example**.

Your tasks:

1) Understand the missed optimization from my single example.
2) Generalize it into a reusable pattern and summarize the pattern so it could be implemented as an InstCombine fold.
3) Generate many additional LLVM IR test cases that are semantically equivalent and should trigger the same transformation, including corner cases.

## Reference: common missed elements and patterns

When generalizing, consider the following summary of elements frequently missed in InstCombine and patterns that are often not well handled. 

The original test exposes a missed fold for a hand-written **floor signed division** idiom.  
For `x / 2`, codegen produced:

- `sdiv x, 2` for truncating signed divide,
- plus a correction term `sext(icmp eq (x & -127), -127)`,

which adds `-1` exactly when `x` is negative and odd. That converts truncating division into **floor division**, so the whole expression is equivalent to `ashr x, 1`.

The patch generalizes this in `foldAddToAshr()` by recognizing the addend as a **rounding/correction mask** attached to `sdiv X, C` where `C` is a power of 2. It extends the matcher from the old canonical form:

- `sext (icmp ugt (X & Mask), SMIN)`

to also accept the equivalent `eq` form:

- `sext (icmp eq (X & Mask), Mask)`

with validation depending on the compare:
- `ugt`: requires `Mask == SMIN | (DivC - 1)`  
- `eq`: currently valid for `DivC == 2`, requiring `Mask == SMIN + 1`

If matched, it folds:
`(X / C) + correction -> ashr X, log2(C)`.

The dev tests broaden coverage from the single i8 case to:
- the original `/2` `eq` pattern,
- the older generalized `/4` `ugt` pattern,
- negative tests for wrong divisor/mask/cast (`zext` must not match),
- commuted add operands,
- vector types.

That’s a good template: add both **positive canonical variants** and **precise near-miss negatives** when lifting one bug pattern into a reusable combine.

## Output format requirement (strict)

Return **ONLY** JSON (a single JSON object). No Markdown, no extra text.

## What I will provide

- The original LLVM IR snippet (minimal reproducer).
- The target triple/datalayout if relevant. 

## What you must output (in JSON)

Include the following top-level keys (you may add more keys if useful):

- "pattern" (generalized pattern description)
- "testcases" (an array of testcase objects; include only positive cases)

Each testcase object must include at least:

- "name"
- "ir" (the input IR for this case)
- "expected_optimized_ir" (what InstCombine should produce, at least the key simplified instructions; full function preferred)

---

## Here is my single example 

define i8 @test(i8 %a) {
  %q.i = sdiv i8 %a, 2
  %1 = and i8 %a, -127
  %_8.i = icmp eq i8 %1, -127
  %2 = sext i1 %_8.i to i8
  %_0.0.i = add nsw i8 %q.i, %2
  ret i8 %_0.0.i
}

define i8 @test(i8 %a) {
  %_0 = ashr i8 %a, 1
  ret i8 %_0
}

pattern.summary: Recognize floor-division-by-power-of-two idioms expressed as truncating signed division plus a sign/oddness correction term, and fold them to arithmetic right shift.

type_constraints: DivC is a positive power-of-two integer constant splat
sext result type equals sdiv result type
all operations use the same integer element width

pattern.semantic_intent: The addend is -1 exactly when X is negative and has any non-zero truncated remainder for division by DivC; adding it to truncating signed division converts it to floor division. For power-of-two divisors, floor signed division equals arithmetic right shift by log2(DivC).

recognized_forms.condition: Currently valid for DivC == 2 with Mask == SMIN + 1 and CmpConst == Mask

pattern.rewrite: (sdiv X, DivC) + sext(correction_i1) --> ashr X, log2(DivC)
notes: The original example is the eq-form for DivC = 2: and X, (SMIN+1); icmp eq with the same mask; sext to all-ones-or-zero; add to sdiv.
This is valid lane-wise for vectors with constant-splat divisor and mask.

testcases.name: scalar_i8_eq_div2_original
testcases.ir:
define i8 @scalar_i8_eq_div2_original(i8 %a) {
  %q = sdiv i8 %a, 2
  %m = and i8 %a, -127
  %c = icmp eq i8 %m, -127
  %adj = sext i1 %c to i8
  %r = add nsw i8 %q, %adj
  ret i8 %r
}

testcases.name: scalar_i8_eq_div2_commuted_add
testcases.ir:
define i8 @scalar_i8_eq_div2_commuted_add(i8 %a) {
  %q = sdiv i8 %a, 2
  %m = and i8 %a, -127
  %c = icmp eq i8 %m, -127
  %adj = sext i1 %c to i8
  %r = add i8 %adj, %q
  ret i8 %r
}

  %c = icmp eq i16 %m, -32767
  %adj = sext i1 %c to i16
  %r = add i16 %q, %adj
  ret i16 %r
}

  %adj = sext i1 %c to i32

testcases.name: vector_v4i8_eq_div2
testcases.ir:
define <4 x i8> @vector_v4i8_eq_div2(<4 x i8> %a) {
  %q = sdiv <4 x i8> %a, <i8 2, i8 2, i8 2, i8 2>
  %m = and <4 x i8> %a, <i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127>
  %c = icmp eq <4 x i8> %m, <i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127>
  %adj = sext <4 x i1> %c to <4 x i8>
  %r = add <4 x i8> %q, %adj
  ret <4 x i8> %r
}

  %q = sdiv i8 %a, 2
  %m = and i8 %a, -127
  %c = icmp eq i8 %m, -127
  %adj = sext i1 %c to i8
  %r = add nsw i8 %q, %adj

----------------------------------------
define i8 @scalar_i8_eq_div2_original(i8 %a) {
#0:
  %q = sdiv i8 %a, 2
  %m = and i8 %a, 129
  %c = icmp eq i8 %m, 129
  %adj = sext i1 %c to i8
  %r = add nsw i8 %q, %adj
  ret i8 %r
}
=>
define i8 @scalar_i8_eq_div2_original(i8 %a) {
#0:
  %r = ashr i8 %a, 1
  ret i8 %r
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

define <4 x i8> @vector_v4i8_eq_div2(<4 x i8> %a) {
  %q = sdiv <4 x i8> %a, <i8 2, i8 2, i8 2, i8 2>
  %m = and <4 x i8> %a, <i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127>
  %c = icmp eq <4 x i8> %m, <i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127>
  %adj = sext <4 x i1> %c to <4 x i8>
  %r = add <4 x i8> %q, %adj
  ret <4 x i8> %r
}

opt_to_expected.src:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define <4 x i8> @vector_v4i8_eq_div2(<4 x i8> %a) {
  %q = sdiv <4 x i8> %a, <i8 2, i8 2, i8 2, i8 2>
  %m = and <4 x i8> %a, <i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127>
  %c = icmp eq <4 x i8> %m, <i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127>
  %adj = sext <4 x i1> %c to <4 x i8>
  %r = add nsw <4 x i8> %q, %adj
  ret <4 x i8> %r
}

source_filename = "<stdin>"

  %m = and <4 x i8> %a, <i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127>
  %c = icmp eq <4 x i8> %m, <i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127, i8 -127>
  %adj = sext <4 x i1> %c to <4 x i8>
  %r = add nsw <4 x i8> %q, %adj
  ret <4 x i8> %r
}
